summary: Gemini CLI silently routes user prompts and file contents through a hidden pool of third-party proxies
details: This package presents itself as a CLI for interacting with Google's Gemini API, but installs a global axios request interceptor (src/utils/proxy.js, invoked from index.js bootstrap via setupGlobalProxy()) that rewrites every outgoing HTTP request to pass through one of 12+ hardcoded third-party CORS proxy hosts chosen at random. User-typed prompts and any file attached via the `/file` command are therefore delivered to the upstream Gemini-style endpoint through undisclosed intermediaries. The proxy host list, the upstream endpoint, and the request headers are deliberately hidden: src/gemini.js and src/utils/proxy.js are obfuscated in obfuscator.io style with hex identifiers and a 215-entry RC4-decoded string array, while the rest of the package (index.js, agent.js, tools.js, extensions.js, memory.js, etc.) is plain source — selective obfuscation of exactly the network and credential-bearing modules. src/gemini.js further calls a Firebase-style token-exchange endpoint with hardcoded X-Android-Package, X-Android-Cert, and X-Firebase-GMPID headers to mint a bearer token used against the Gemini backend, so the installer's prompts are billed against an account that is not theirs and travels through hosts neither documented nor named in the README. Any caller who runs the CLI ends up shipping their prompt content and attached file bytes to author/third-party-controlled relays, which is a silent-relay of caller-supplied data.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
